hero.question: Will the Fed decrease interest rates by 25 bps after the April 2026 meeting?
hero.prob_take: The 3-point dump in 24 hours signals traders are pricing in stickier inflation or hawkish Fed forward guidance, erasing whatever small chance existed for an April 2026 cut. At 5.5%, this is essentially dead money, but it's also too cheap if there's even a 10-15% baseline probability the Fed overshoots on rates and panic-cuts by mid-2026.

daily_take.headline: The Fed Rate Cut Myth: Why April 2026 Has Become a Sucker Bet
daily_take.deck: The odds for a 25 bps cut post-April 2026 cratered 3 points to 5.5%, and the market finally got it right. The Fed isn't cutting in April 2026 unless the economy implodes between now and then, which would require a scenario so catastrophic that betting on it at 5.5% is just burning money. The real play: fade the hopium. Anyone touching this under 3% is playing with fire; smart money already left the building.
